* feat(skills): extract Get-AllPRsWithComments to GitHubCore module

Move the GraphQL PR query function from scripts/Update-ReviewerSignalStats.ps1
into .claude/skills/github/modules/GitHubCore.psm1 for reuse across skills.

Changes:
- Add Get-AllPRsWithComments to GitHubCore API Helpers section

- Export function from module

- Add Pester tests covering parameter validation, pagination, date filtering,
  comment filtering, and error handling

Closes #872

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove duplicate mock in Get-AllPRsWithComments.Tests.ps1

Remove dead code where a mock on line 136 was immediately overwritten
by another mock on lines 137-140. The first mock used shorthand syntax
while the second used named parameters with LASTEXITCODE handling.
All other tests in this file use only the second pattern.

* fix(tests): use script scope for pagination test counter

The pagination test used $callCount without the $script: scope modifier.
Due to PowerShell scoping rules, $callCount++ inside the mock scriptblock
creates a local variable each invocation instead of modifying the outer
variable. This meant the condition $callCount -eq 1 was always true,
so the else branch (returning page 2) never executed.

Changed to $script:callCount to ensure the counter persists across
mock invocations, matching the pattern used in Add-CommentReaction.Tests.ps1.

* fix(security): use Invoke-GhGraphQL for parameterized queries

Address GraphQL injection vulnerability in Get-AllPRsWithComments by
replacing direct string interpolation with Invoke-GhGraphQL helper.
Owner, Repo, and cursor are now passed as GraphQL variables.

Changes:
- Replace raw gh api graphql call with Invoke-GhGraphQL
- Add ValidateRange(1, MaxValue) to MaxPages parameter
- Update TOC line reference for API Helpers section
- Rewrite tests to mock Invoke-GhGraphQL instead of gh
- Add injection prevention tests verifying parameterization
- Add MaxPages validation tests for 0 and negative values

Addresses review comments from gemini-code-assist and copilot.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(skills): prevent array unwrapping in Get-AllPRsWithComments return

Use comma operator to preserve array type when returning single-element
results from Get-AllPRsWithComments. PowerShell unwraps single-element
arrays to scalars on function return, causing test assertions on
$result[0].number to return $null instead of the PR number.

Fixes: "Filters out PRs without review comments" test
Fixes: "Stops pagination when PRs are older than Since date" test

test: close test coverage gaps across config, github, mcp, and daemon modules (#1)

* chore: add tempfile dev-dependency for config test fixtures

Needed for tests that write temporary YAML config files and create
temporary directories to exercise DaemonConfig::load and validate_binaries.

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

* test(config): add comprehensive unit tests for DaemonConfig

Catches: YAML parsing regressions, incorrect defaults, missing tilde
expansion, broken CLI arg generation for webserver/indexer, incorrect
binary path resolution, and serde round-trip failures.

Coverage gaps closed:
- DaemonConfig::load (valid YAML, invalid YAML, missing file, empty file)
- DaemonConfig::expand_paths (tilde expansion, no-op for absolute paths)
- DaemonConfig::build_path (with/without custom bin directories)
- DaemonConfig::webserver_args (default flags, custom flags, pprof, log_dir)
- DaemonConfig::indexer_args (ctags on/off/require, delta, branches, large_files)
- DaemonConfig::indexer_bin / webserver_bin (with/without zoekt_bin)
- Default values for DaemonConfig, CtagsConfig, WebserverConfig
- OwnerKind default variant
- GitHub config deserialization from YAML
- Serde round-trip (serialize → deserialize)

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

* test(github): add filter_repos, edge-case, and async resolve tests

Catches: repos incorrectly passing through archived/fork/exclude filters,
wildcard pattern matching regressions (empty patterns, consecutive stars,
both-end wildcards, partial matches without wildcards), and resolve_all_repos
short-circuit logic when GitHub config is absent or has no sources.

Coverage gaps closed:
- filter_repos (skip_archived, skip_forks, exclude patterns, combined, empty)
- matches_pattern edge cases (empty pattern, multiple/consecutive wildcards,
  both-end wildcard, exact substring without wildcard)
- is_excluded (empty patterns list, exact match pattern)
- resolve_all_repos (no GitHub config, empty sources, empty explicit list)

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

* test(daemon): add validate_binaries boundary tests

Catches: validate_binaries incorrectly erroring when zoekt_bin is None
(should always pass), failing to detect missing binaries in an explicit
directory, and false negatives when dummy binaries exist.

Coverage gaps closed:
- validate_binaries with no zoekt_bin (None → Ok)
- validate_binaries with nonexistent directory path → Err
- validate_binaries with real directory missing binaries → Err
- validate_binaries with directory containing both binaries → Ok

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

* test(mcp): add unit tests for formatting, base64, and serde types

Catches: base64 decoding regressions (empty, valid, invalid UTF-8, plain
text fallback), formatting bugs in all output modes (content with chunk
matches and line matches, files_with_matches, count, repos), symbol info
rendering edge cases (missing kind/parent), and JSON serialization/
deserialization failures for all Zoekt API types.

Coverage gaps closed:
- decode_b64 (empty, valid base64, plain text fallback, invalid UTF-8)
- format_files (empty/None files, populated files)
- format_count (empty, chunk matches, line matches, no-matches-in-file)
- format_repos (empty/None, with data, symbols flag, version truncation)
- format_content (empty, chunk matches with ranges, symbol info with/
  without kind/parent, line matches with/without before/after context,
  no language tag)
- SearchRequest/ListRequest serialization (PascalCase field renames,
  skip_serializing_if for opts)
- SearchResponse/ListResponse deserialization (default fields, null files)
- FileMatch/ChunkMatch/LineMatch deserialization (default values)

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

commit.message:
test: close test coverage gaps across all modules (59 → 125 tests) (#1)

* test(ir): add Glyph coverage for PUT/PATCH/unknown methods, as_char all variants, serde roundtrips

Catches bugs in:
- Glyph::from_methods for PUT, PATCH, and unknown HTTP methods (HEAD, OPTIONS, TRACE)
- Glyph::as_char returning wrong unicode for any variant
- Glyph serde serialization/deserialization losing variant identity
- Hash collisions between Glyph variants
- CompletionSpec JSON roundtrip losing data
- Empty group vectors not surviving serde

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

* test(spec): add PathItemExt coverage for all HTTP methods and FileSpecLoader error paths

Catches bugs in:
- PathItemExt::operations() missing PUT/PATCH/DELETE methods
- PathItemExt::operations() ordering guarantee across all 5 methods
- PathItemExt::operations() on items with no operations
- FileSpecLoader failing silently on missing files
- FileSpecLoader accepting invalid YAML/JSON without error
- Path-level parameter parsing regressions

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

* test(convert): cover Auto→ByOperationId, empty IDs, body field edge cases, duplicate flags

Catches bugs in:
- Auto strategy incorrectly falling to ByPath when operationIds exist but tags don't
- operation_id_group returning wrong value for empty strings
- collect_body_fields not early-returning for non-JSON media types
- collect_body_fields not early-returning when schema is None
- collect_body_fields misbehaving on empty properties
- Duplicate flag deduplication losing first-wins semantics
- Description falling back to info.title vs info.description
- Single-op group using formatted description instead of operation summary
- strip_verb_prefix missing coverage for add/set/put/patch/post/find/search/fetch
- path_group and op_name edge cases (param-only paths, nested paths)

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

* test(gen): cover generate() dispatcher for all formats, directory creation, trait impls

Catches bugs in:
- generate() not creating output directory tree
- generate() returning wrong number of files for Format::All
- Format::All not producing both .yaml and .fish files
- SkimTabGenerator/FishGenerator format_name() mismatches
- Trait dispatch (OutputGenerator) not routing to correct generator
- Format::from_str_loose case sensitivity and alias coverage

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

* test(fish): cover shell_quote edge cases and multi-group/multi-alias generation

commit.message:
Add Pipeline Parallelism support for DeepSeek V3 (#559)

## Summary
- Adds pipeline parallelism (PP) support for DeepSeek V3 model training
- Implements custom pipeline schedules (1F1B, Interleaved 1F1B)
optimized for large MoE models
- Adds pipeline stage management for splitting models across multiple
GPUs
- Updates dataloaders to handle incomplete microbatches when PP is
enabled (drop_last=True)
- Supports multiple pipeline stages per GPU for better utilization

## Key Changes
- New pipeline_parallelism/ module with pipeline_model.py,
pipeline_schedules.py, pipeline_stage.py
- New pipelining_utils.py for PP helper functions
- Updated parallelize.py for DeepSeek V3 to support PP
- Modified trainer and worker classes to handle model_parts list when PP
is enabled
- Added PP-specific configuration options

## Test plan
- [x] Test SFT training with PP enabled on DeepSeek V3
- [x] Verify correct gradient computation across pipeline stages
- [x] Test with various PP configurations (pp_size=2, 4, etc.)
- [x] Verify checkpoint save/load works correctly with PP

These changes were adapted from @gekurian 's implementation of PP in the
imaginiare4 repository:
https://gitlab-master.nvidia.com/dir/imaginaire4/-/tree/gekurian/ds1k/projects/cosmos/reasoning/v1/parallelisms?ref_type=heads

Results on H100 with the DeepSeek V3 model
Common config: tp=1, ep=64, dp_shard=64, train_batch_per_replica=8,
model_max_length=512, minibatch=8

Config | Avg (s) | Speedup vs CP=4
-- | -- | --
CP=4 baseline | 9.32 | baseline
PP4-mbs2-GPipe | 7.08 | 1.32x
PP4-mbs2-I1F1B | 6.58 | 1.42x
PP4-mbs2-I1F1B-lps2 | 6.50 | 1.43x
PP4-mbs2-I1F1B-lps4 | 6.71 | 1.39x
PP4-mbs1-I1F1B | 10.29 | 0.91x
PP2-mbs1-I1F1B | 15.46 | 0.60x
PP8-mbs1-I1F1B | 7.15 | 1.30x

* fix: SubnetCidrDistribution raises ConstraintError with wrong arity

The constructor for SubnetCidrDistribution was calling
`raise Dry::Types::ConstraintError, message` but ConstraintError
requires two arguments (message, input). This caused a confusing
CoercionError instead of the intended ConstraintError with a
descriptive message.

Also fixes LoadBalancingConfig which had a required `type` field
but a `.default({}.freeze)` that would always fail validation.
Changed `type` to optional (`type?`) to match the default behavior.

Bug class: incorrect error types masking validation failures

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

* fix: SecureVpc types use .enum().default() which is unsupported

dry-types requires `.default(value).enum(*values)` ordering, not
`.enum(*values).default(value)`. Fixed flow_log_destination and
instance_tenancy attribute declarations.

Bug class: type definition load-time crashes preventing module usage

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

* fix: PublicPrivateSubnets nat_gateway_type uses .enum().default()

Same dry-types ordering issue: `.enum('single', 'per_az').default('per_az')`

commit.message:
test: close coverage gaps across 8 modules (+86 tests) and fix topological sort bug (#1)

* test(flake): add compute_update_chain tests and fix topological sort bug

Add 11 tests for compute_update_chain covering:
- Empty/no-dependent inputs (boundary conditions)
- Single and transitive dependencies (correct ordering)
- Diamond dependency graphs (correct in-degree handling)
- Cycle detection (error path)
- Deep linear chains (traversal correctness)
- Multiple inputs per repo (partial update selection)

Bug fix: the Kahn's algorithm in-degree calculation incorrectly counted
edges from the `changed` trigger node (which is not part of the
topological sort). These edges inflated in-degree counts that were never
decremented, causing false 'cycle detected' errors for any non-trivial
dependency graph. Fixed by only counting ordering edges between affected
repos.

conditional inclusion of bare metal BGP fields.

Tests coverage gaps in BareMetal::CloudInit.generate:
- Dynamic node_index: IMDSv2 snippet generation, sed replacement
- ArgoCD config passthrough: inclusion/exclusion, key stringification
- VPN config passthrough: conditional inclusion
- Secrets/bootstrap_secrets passthrough: conditional inclusion
- k3s/kubernetes config passthrough: conditional inclusion
- Role normalization: server→control-plane, agent→worker for kubernetes
- cloud_config format: correct path, permissions, valid JSON

- Shell script security: set -euo pipefail, mkdir -p, chmod 0640

or where argocd/vpn/secrets configs are silently dropped.

Tests all provider-specific result type subclasses that had no coverage:
- NetworkResult: AWS fields (igw, route_table, etcd_bucket, flow_log, kms_key)
- GcpNetworkResult: firewall_internal/external
- AzureNetworkResult: resource_group, vnet, nsg
- HcloudNetworkResult: network
- IamResult: AWS fields (log_group, ecr_policy, karpenter_role/profile)
- AwsEksIamResult: cluster_role, node_role, parent field fallback
- GcpIamResult: node_sa

Tests hash-style [] access, to_h conditional inclusion, nil field pruning,
and is_a? contract compliance (all subclasses pass Pangea::Contracts checks).

Catches bugs where [] accessor misroutes a key or where to_h includes nil
fields that would generate invalid Terraform JSON.

Co-authored-by: drzzln <drzzln@protonmail.com>

Tests canonical name resolution (aws_eks, aws_nixos_k3s, gcp_gke, etc.),
legacy alias resolution (aws→aws_eks, hcloud→hcloud_k3s, etc.), and
all_names method (union of canonical + alias keys with no duplicates).

Validates that every alias in ALIASES points to a valid BACKEND_MAP entry,
and that alias/canonical pairs resolve to the same backend module.

Catches bugs where a new alias is added but points to a non-existent
canonical name, or where canonical resolution breaks after adding aliases.

backend_contract_spec: Tests Base module contract enforcement (NotImplementedError
for backend_name/compatible_backends, defaults for mesh_capable?, l7_observable?,

implementations including Cilium Helm values (Hubble, ENI/overlay modes,

compatibility_spec: Tests NetworkBackendRegistry edge cases — alias resolution
(eni→vpc_cni, aws_cni→vpc_cni, ebpf→cilium), NixOS backend compatibility

Tests build_bootstrap_secrets, build_agent_bootstrap_secrets, and
build_secrets_hash methods that control what credentials reach cloud-init.

Key coverage:
- build_bootstrap_secrets: nil/empty/all-empty-strings → nil (no empty secrets)

  nix_github_token — never leaks sops_age_key or flux_github_token to workers

commit.message:
feat(cmd-k): Add 'superuser' keyword to admin actions (#112298) ## Description Adds the `superuser` keyword to all admin CMD-K actions and restores the "Open Superuser Modal" action from the old command palette to match the old workflow and improve discoverability. Also updates the organization admin URL to use the correct customers endpoint. This addresses the issue where users were trying to search for "superuser" in the new CMD-K but couldn't find the admin actions or the superuser escalation modal. ## Changes - Added `keywords: [t('superuser')]` to "Open _admin" action - Added `keywords: [t('superuser')]` to "Open org in _admin" action - Added `keywords: [t('superuser')]` to "Exit Superuser" action - **Added "Open Superuser Modal" action** - allows staff users to escalate to superuser mode (only shown when not already a superuser) - Changed organization admin URL from `/_admin/organizations/${organization.slug}/` to `/_admin/customers/${organization.slug}/` ## Testing Users can now type "superuser" in CMD-K and see: - "Open _admin" - opens the Django admin interface - "Open org in _admin" - opens the organization's customer page in Django admin - "Open Superuser Modal" - opens the authentication modal to escalate to superuser (only shown when not already superuser) - "Exit Superuser" - exits superuser mode (only shown when already superuser) [Slack Thread](https://sentry.slack.com/archives/C08QLT0PYQK/p1775510843648239?thread_ts=1775510843.648239&cid=C08QLT0PYQK)
  
--------- Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent Co-authored-by: Jonas

commit.message:
Harden pnpm supply chain security (#149)

* harden pnpm supply chain security

- Move core-js from onlyBuiltDependencies to ignoredBuiltDependencies
  (its postinstall only prints donation messages, not needed for builds)
- Add blockExoticSubdeps: true to prevent transitive deps from using
  git repos or direct tarball URLs
- Add minimumReleaseAge: 10080 (1 week) to delay installing newly
  published versions, giving time for malware detection
- Add trustPolicy: no-downgrade to prevent installing packages whose
  trust level has decreased compared to previous releases

Co-authored-by: Jared Rhizor <me@jaredrhizor.com>

* respect delay in dependabot

* use exclustions and trustPolicyIgnoreAfter

Super-admin tenant impersonation with RLS, audit, and dev auto-attach (#4)

* Add tenant impersonation migration and audit table types

Introduce JWT-driven effective tenant for super_admin via app_metadata,
RLS helpers, tenant_impersonation_audit, and updated policies for core
tenant-scoped tables plus read-only enforcement while impersonating.

* Add impersonation API routes and client helpers

POST /api/impersonation/start and /end validate super_admin, update JWT
app_metadata, and write tenant_impersonation_audit. Server uses service
role; client helpers call the API then refresh the session.

* Wire tenant context and UI for super-admin impersonation

Resolve effective tenant_id from JWT impersonation; refresh on token
update after API start/end. Expose homeTenantId, impersonation state, and
actions. Allow super_admin without home tenant to load app; banner and
Admin Tenants View-as-tenant; optional dev auto-impersonate on create.

* Add migration to extend tenant_role enum with super_admin

Fixes invalid input value for enum tenant_role when applying RLS that
references super_admin; run before tenant impersonation migration.
